sourceMovie.title: The Punisher
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Punisher (2004) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Léon: The Professional
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Punisher clicked for you, Léon: The Professional (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Death Wish
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Punisher clicked for you, Death Wish (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Punisher?
faq.answer: Try Léon: The Professional, Death Wish, The Enforcer—each shares DNA with The Punisher while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Punisher?
faq.answer: The Punisher (2004) is best described as Action, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Punisher part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Punisher Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
